seoTitle: How to Make Buttons With Icons in Webflow
metaDescription: Build a clean call-to-action button with an inline icon in Webflow using a Link Block and flexbox alignment properties.
intro: Pairing an icon with your button text, like an arrow next to 'Learn More', gives a CTA extra visual polish, but aligning them properly takes flexbox know-how. This guide covers nesting a text element and an SVG icon inside a Link Block and using flexbox to keep everything centered and evenly spaced. It's for designers who want interactive buttons without writing custom CSS.

steps.heading: Add a Link Block
steps.body: Open the Add Elements panel and drag a Link Block onto the canvas to serve as your button container.

steps.heading: Nest a text block and icon
steps.body: Add a Text Block for the button label and upload or embed a small SVG icon inside the same Link Block.

steps.heading: Turn on flexbox layout
steps.body: In the right sidebar style panel, apply a horizontal flexbox layout preset to the Link Block so its children sit in a row.

steps.heading: Center align and add spacing
steps.body: Set vertical alignment to center so the text and icon line up, then add a small gap between them for visual breathing room.

steps.heading: Style hover states and finalize
steps.body: Apply a hover state transition for interactivity, assign the styling to a reusable global class, then test across breakpoints.

faq.question: Why aren't my text and icon aligning vertically?
faq.answer: This usually means flexbox alignment isn't set to center on the parent Link Block, so check that setting first.

faq.question: Can I reuse this button style elsewhere on my site?
faq.answer: Yes, assigning a global class to the Link Block lets you apply the same button design consistently across pages.

faq.question: What file type should my icon be?
faq.answer: SVG is the recommended format since it scales cleanly at any size without losing quality.

faq.question: How do I add a hover effect to the button?
faq.answer: Use Webflow's built-in hover state in the style panel to adjust color, spacing, or a subtle transition on mouse-over.
